_comment: Lighthouse CI runs against the storefront with the SMMTA-NEXT API
stubbed → catalogue read returns empty, no real product images load,
every run is cold (no warm caches), and headless Chrome occasionally
fails to gather one of the metrics outright (score reported as NaN).
This means assertions on category scores are inherently flaky in CI
and would block PRs on noise rather than real regressions.
- assertions: `warn` (logged in CI, never block the PR)
- upload: still happens, so the per-PR report URL is visible in
the workflow logs and reviewers can spot trends
- numberOfRuns: 3, so the median dampens single-shot noise
The real perf target (per Prompt 13) is Performance / SEO /
Accessibility >= 0.95 against the live storefront with real
catalogue + CDN-cached images. That target is checked manually
against staging before each release; CI is for catching obvious
breakage. Once we ship a CI harness that boots the API + Postgres
and seeds a populated catalogue, these can be promoted from `warn`
